title: [TASK] Set up Docker Compose for local development orchestration

conversation_log.summary: Task is fully consistent with architecture.md and ADR-004. File locations (docker/docker-compose.yml, docker/Dockerfile.frontend, docker/Dockerfile.backend) match the canonical folder structure. Service definitions (frontend, backend, db) align with the architecture overview. Environment variable naming follows SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention. .env.example placement at repo root matches the environment configuration pattern. Dev-only Dockerfiles respect the local-development-only constraint. Volume mounts reference correct monorepo paths (apps/frontend, apps/backend). Only backend connects to PostgreSQL, preserving the access pattern rule.
Requirements Specialist marked this issue as a duplicate of #10. Canonical task with full conversation history is #10. All sign-off activity to continue on #10.
Tester acknowledged duplicate status. No testability review performed on #17. All acceptance criteria review activity is on canonical task issue #10. No further action on this issue.

Implemented Docker Compose local development orchestration. Created docker/docker-compose.yml (3 services: frontend, backend, db), docker/Dockerfile.frontend, docker/Dockerfile.backend, .env.example at repo root, .gitignore, and README.md with Local Development section. PR #18 opened.

decisions.approach: Created all Docker infrastructure files as specified by AC #1-15. Used postgres:16 official image for db service with pg_isready healthcheck. Backend depends on db with service_healthy condition to prevent premature startup. Anonymous volume overrides prevent host node_modules from clobbering container-installed ones. curl installed in both Dockerfiles to enable AC #7 connectivity verification. All sensitive values sourced exclusively from .env — no hardcoded credentials anywhere.
risks: apps/frontend and apps/backend directories must exist (Feature #1 monorepo task) before docker compose up is fully functional — bind-mounts will create empty directories otherwise.
Root workspace package.json, turbo.json, and tsconfig.json must exist before Docker images can be built successfully — provided by the monorepo setup task.

The db service name is consistently 'db' throughout all 15 ACs in issue #17; DATABASE_URL in .env.example uses hostname 'db' accordingly. Note: issue #10's Architect suggested renaming to 'postgres', but issue #17 consistently uses 'db' in the finalized ACs and was the assigned implementation task.
